title: Passaging of cells
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1u2jn","text":"Warm up culture media, PBS and Trypsin in a   ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":44,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"4vpra","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"ED16C200CA0D11EC86DD273E1EB24A79","id":0,"label":"Waterbath","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"37","unit":10}}}}
steps: Inspect cells for confluency, when 70-90 % confluency is reached cells are ready for passage
Discard media and wash once with PBS
Add Trypsin to fully cover the surface of the culture vessel
Lightly tap the culture vessel and check for cell detachement. If cells are still attached prolong incubation
Add 2/3 of culture media to 1/3 of trypsin and transfer cells into a Falcon tube
Centrifuge at 300 rcf, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Discard supernatant and resubstitute with 1 mL, culture media
Count cells and adjust media volume for required cell density
creator: Guido Krähenbühl
